volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Seven against Thebes (Section 800 - Section 1.967)

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: τὰς δ ἑβδόμας ὁ σεμνὸς ἑβδομαγέτης
sections.translation: But lord Apollo , the reverend leader of the seventh , [*] took for himself the seventh gate , accomplishing upon the children of Oedipus the ancient follies of Laius .

sections.label: Section 803
sections.original: τί δ ἔστι πρᾶγμα νεόκοτον πόλει πλέον ;
sections.translation: What novel happening will further affect the city ?

sections.label: Section 804
sections.translation: The city is saved , but the kings born of the same seed —

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: τίνες ; τί δ εἶπας ; παραφρονῶ φόβῳ λόγου .
sections.translation: Who ? What did you say ? I am out of my mind with fear of your report .

sections.label: Section 806
sections.translation: Control yourself now and listen . The sons of Oedipus —

sections.label: Section 807
sections.original: οἲ ʼγὼ τάλαινα , μάντις εἰμὶ τῶν κακῶν .
sections.translation: Ah , miserable me , I am prophet of these evils .

sections.label: Section 808
sections.translation: In truth , beyond all question , struck down in the dust —

sections.label: Section 809
sections.original: ἐκεῖθι κεῖσθον ; [*] βαρέα δ οὖν ὅμως φράσον .
sections.translation: Are they lying out there ? This is hard to bear , but say it just the same .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.translation: The men are dead , murdered by their very own hands .

sections.label: Section 811
sections.translation: Then with hands so fraternal did they each kill the other together ?

sections.label: Section 812
sections.original: πέπωκεν αἷμα γαῖ [*] ὑπ ἀλλήλων φόνῳ .
sections.translation: Yes , so all too equal was their destiny to them both . All alone , in truth , it consumes the ill-fated family . We have cause in this for joy and tears —

sections.label: Section 815
sections.translation: the one because the city fares well , the other because the leaders , the two generals , have divided the whole of their property with hammered Scythian steel . They will possess only that land they take in burial , swept away as they were in accordance with their father ’ s curses .

sections.label: Section 820
sections.translation: The city is saved , but through their mutual murder the earth has drunk the blood of the two kings born of the same seed . Exit .

sections.label: Section 822
sections.translation: O great Zeus and the divine powers that guard our city , you who indeed protect these walls of Cadmus ,

sections.label: Section 825
sections.translation: should I rejoice and shout in triumph for the unharmed safety of the city , or should I lament our leaders in war ,

sections.label: Section 830
sections.translation: now wretched , ill-fated and childless ? Indeed , in exact accordance with their name and as men of much strife , they have perished through their impious intent .

sections.label: Section 1.833
sections.translation: O black curse on the family , Oedipus ’ curse , now brought to fulfillment ! A chill of horror falls about my heart .

sections.label: Section 1.835
sections.translation: In frenzy like a maenad I make my song for the grave as I hear of their corpses dripping with blood , how they died through the workings of cruel fate . This song of the spear , sung to the flute , is indeed born of an ill omen . [*]

sections.label: Section 1.840
sections.translation: The curseful utterance of their father has done its work and not fallen short . Laius ’ plans , made in disobedience , have kept their force . I am anxious for our city ; divine decrees do not lose their edge . The funeral procession with the bodies of the brothers comes into view .

sections.label: Section 1.845
sections.translation: O bringers of immense grief , you have done in this a deed beyond belief , yet lamentable troubles have indeed come .

sections.label: Section 1.848
sections.translation: The events are self-evident ; the messenger ’ s report is plain to see . Twofold is our distress — double disaster

sections.label: Section 1.850
sections.translation:
of kindred murder , this double suffering has come to fulfillment . What shall I say ? What else indeed than that sorrow born of sorrows surround this house ’ s hearth ?
But sail upon the wind of lamentation , my friends ,

sections.label: Section 1.855
sections.translation: and about your head row with your hands ’ rapid stroke in conveyance of the dead , [*] that stroke which always causes the sacred slack-sailed , black-clothed ship to pass over Acheron to the unseen land where Apollo does not walk ,

sections.label: Section 1.860
sections.translation:
the sunless land that receives all men .
But here come Antigone and Ismene to do their bitter duty , the dirge over their brothers both . With all sincerity , I think , will they

sections.label: Section 1.865
sections.translation: pour forth their fitting grief from their lovely , deep-bosomed breasts . But it is right for us , before their singing , to cry out the awful hymn of the Erinys and thereafter

sections.label: Section 1.870
sections.translation:
sing the hated victory song of Hades .
Ah , sisters most unfortunate in your kin of all women who clasp their girdle about their robes , I weep , I groan , and there is no feigning in the shrill cries that come straight from my heart .

sections.label: Section 1.875
sections.translation: Ah , pity you senseless men , whom friends could not persuade and evils could not wear down ! To your misery you have captured your father ’ s house with the spear .

sections.label: Section 1.879
sections.translation: To their misery , indeed ,

sections.label: Section 1.880
sections.translation: they found a miserable death in the outrage done their house .

sections.label: Section 1.881
sections.translation: Ah , you brothers who were poised to cast over the walls of your home and looked — to your sorrow — for sole rule , now you have been

sections.label: Section 1.885
sections.translation:
reconciled by the iron sword .
The great Erinys of your father Oedipus has fulfilled it all truly .

sections.label: Section 2.888
sections.translation: Pierced through your left sides , pierced indeed —

sections.label: Section 2.890
sections.translation: through those sides that were born from one womb ! Ah , strange ones ! Ah , the curses that demand death for death !

sections.label: Section 2.895
sections.translation: Right through , as you say , were they struck , with blows to house and body by an unspeakable wrath and by the doom , called down by their father ’ s curse , which they shared without discord .

sections.label: Section 2.900
sections.translation: Groaning spreads throughout the city , too : the walls groan ; the land that loves its sons groans . But for those who come after them there remains their property , on which account the strife

sections.label: Section 2.905
sections.translation:
of those terrible-fated men came to fulfillment in death .
In their haste to anger they apportioned their property so that each has an equal share . To those who loved them their reconciler is not blameless ,

sections.label: Section 2.910
sections.translation: nor is Ares agreeable .

sections.label: Section 3.911
sections.translation: Under strokes of iron they are come to this , and under strokes of iron there await them — what , one might perhaps ask — shares in their father ’ s tomb . [*]

sections.label: Section 3.915
sections.translation: Our shrill , heart-rending wail goes with them — product of lamentation and pain felt of its own accord — a wail from a distressed mind , joyless , pouring forth tears from a heart

sections.label: Section 3.920
sections.translation: that wastes away as I weep for these two princes .

sections.label: Section 3.922
sections.translation: Over these poor men it can be said that they did much to harm our citizens and also the ranks of all the foreigners

sections.label: Section 3.925
sections.translation:
who died in abundance in the fighting .
Ill-fated beyond all women who are called by the name of mother is she who bore them . After she made her own child her own husband ,

sections.label: Section 3.930
sections.original: τούσδ ἔτεχ , οἱ δ ὧδ ἐτελεύ-
sections.translation: she gave birth to these sons , who have thus ended their lives with kindred hands giving death for death .

sections.label: Section 4.933
sections.translation: Of the same seed , in truth , they were utterly destroyed in unloving divisions ,

sections.label: Section 4.935
sections.translation:
in maddened discord , in the ending of their strife .
Their hatred has ceased . Their life has been mingled in the blood-soaked earth . Now truly their blood is one .

sections.label: Section 4.940
sections.translation: Ruthless is that which resolved their strife , the stranger from across the sea , sharpened iron rushed from the fire . Ruthless , too , was Ares , the cruel divider of their property , who made their father ’ s curses come true .

sections.label: Section 4.945
sections.translation:
They hold in misery their allotted portion of god-given sorrows . Beneath their corpses there will be boundless wealth of earth .
Ah , you have wreathed

sections.label: Section 4.950
sections.translation: your race with many troubles ! In the final outcome the Curses have raised their piercing cry , now that the family is turned to flight in all directions . A trophy to Ruin now stands at the gate

sections.label: Section 4.955
sections.original: ἐν αἷς ἐθείνοντο , καὶ δυοῖν κρατή-
sections.translation: where they struck each other and where , having conquered them both , the divine power stayed its hand .

sections.label: Section 957
sections.translation: You were struck as you struck .

sections.label: Section 958
sections.translation: By the spear you killed —

sections.label: Section 959
sections.translation: By the spear you died —

sections.label: Section 960
sections.translation: Your deed made you wretched .

sections.label: Section 961
sections.translation: You suffering made you wretched .

sections.label: Section 962
sections.translation: Let the lament come .

sections.label: Section 963
sections.translation: Let the tears come .

sections.label: Section 964
sections.translation: You are laid out for mourning —

sections.label: Section 965
sections.translation: Though you did the killing .

sections.label: Section 1.966b
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1.967
sections.translation: My heart is mad with wailing .
